Title: Physics-Guided Operational Stiffness Proxies for Reduced-Order Screening of Harmonic Resonance Risk in 33/11 kV Distribution Substations
Scope: In scope: develop and validate a physics-guided, reduced-order Python package that screens harmonic resonance susceptibility in 33/11 kV substations from routine operational measurements (busbar voltage, incomer current, feeder current, feeder power), reconstructs operating regimes, and outputs interpretable probabilistic risk classes with manuscript-ready artifacts. Out of scope: direct harmonic waveform measurement campaigns, full electromagnetic transient simulation, real-time closed-loop control deployment, and hardware-in-the-loop implementation.

Objectives.statement: Define and compute physics-guided proxy indicators that represent voltage margin, loading stress, feeder concentration, dynamics, and operational stiffness from routine substation data.
Reconstruct representative operating regimes using medoid-based clustering of standardized proxy vectors and identify characteristic regime medoids.
Formulate a reduced-order resonance susceptibility score grounded in harmonic amplification physics and operational stiffness approximation.
Train and evaluate an interpretable ordinal probabilistic screening model for low, moderate, and high resonance-risk classes.
Validate robustness and practical transferability through temporal cross-validation, sensitivity analysis, and benchmark comparisons.

Data to be used.name: Primary substation SCADA operational dataset (33/11 kV, hourly or 15-min resolution)
Data to be used.justification: Core dataset required to compute operational proxies, reconstruct regimes, and estimate resonance susceptibility for the target substation context.

Data to be used.name: Open Power System Data - Time Series (benchmark and pipeline hardening)
Data to be used.justification: Used for workflow hardening, schema checks, and reproducibility tests when private utility exports are unavailable in CI.

steps: M01 - Define study design and end-to-end analytical workflow for physics-guided resonance-risk screening.
M02 - Specify study system context and compile data dictionary for 33/11 kV substation variables.
M03 - Harmonize timestamps across monthly sheets and channels into one aligned time index.
M04 - Apply missing-data treatment (Kalman smoothing and seasonal interpolation policy with fallback rules).
M05 - Run outlier screening using MAD-based filters and consistency checks.
M06 - Normalize electrical variables to per-unit values using feeder/incomer ratings.
M07 - Compute busbar voltage depression index (V_dep).
M08 - Compute busbar voltage imbalance index (V_imb).
M09 - Compute incomer utilization and loading concentration indices.
M10 - Compute feeder utilization, feeder coincidence, and feeder imbalance indicators.
M11 - Compute load ramp-rate and feeder ramp-dispersion dynamic indicators.
M12 - Compute voltage-to-load sensitivity stiffness proxy K(t)=|dP/dV| with numerical safeguards.
M13 - Standardize proxy feature space for clustering and modeling.
M14 - Run PAM clustering and tune number of clusters using silhouette and gap-statistic criteria.
M15 - Extract and interpret representative operating-state medoids.
M16 - Construct reduced-order susceptibility score R(t)=V_dep*U_inc*C_inc/K(t).
M17 - Define engineering-grounded ordinal risk labels and threshold rules (low/medium/high).
M18 - Fit interpretable ordinal risk model (cumulative logit; constrained GAM as sensitivity alternative).
M19 - Implement baseline comparators (voltage-threshold, load-threshold, unweighted proxy index).
M20 - Execute time-series cross-validation with leakage-safe temporal folds.
M21 - Perform sensitivity analysis (leave-one-proxy-out and coefficient/effect stability checks).
M22 - Run uncertainty and robustness analysis under threshold and clustering perturbations.
M23 - Compare final model versus benchmarks on discrimination, calibration, and class-wise performance.
M24 - Generate manuscript-grade tables, figures, and reproducibility artifacts from the finalized pipeline.
Methodology & Analysis Plan.mathematical_basis: Harmonic amplification relation V_h = Z_eq(f)*I_h; operational approximation |Z_eq| proportional to 1/K where K is voltage-load sensitivity stiffness proxy; reduced-order susceptibility R(t)=V_dep(t)*U_inc(t)*C_inc(t)/K(t); regime-conditioned expectation R_state = E[R(t)|cluster_j]; ordinal cumulative-logit model for ordered risk categories.

literature_context.provided_notes: The literature notes indicate that MV (33/11 kV-class) substations are increasingly exposed to harmonic distortion, voltage profile stress, unbalance, and resonance-sensitive operating conditions under nonlinear loads and DER growth. Existing resonance approaches are often model-heavy (EMT/frequency-scan) or monitoring-heavy (waveform harmonics), limiting utility-scale deployment. The proposed study is positioned as an operational screening framework rather than direct harmonic measurement, emphasizing interpretable, physics-guided proxies (voltage margin, loading stress, concentration, dynamics, stiffness) that can be computed from routine SCADA measurements. Prior work supports using state-dependent analysis, robust validation, and practical recommendations constrained by data and modelling limits.

key_references.citation: Razmi, D., et al. (2023). An Overview on Power Quality Issues and Control Strategies for Distribution Networks With the Presence of Distributed Generation Resources. IEEE Access.
key_references.relevance: Background on modern PQ stressors in DER-rich distribution networks and harmonic management context.

key_references.relevance: Supports interpretable and data-driven risk analytics framing for operational screening.

key_references.citation: Ansari, H., et al. (2025). Harmonic Resonance Monitoring in Distribution Networks Considering Load Variation and Possible Network Configurations. IET GTD.
key_references.relevance: Motivates load- and configuration-dependent resonance risk and monitoring strategies.

key_references.citation: Shakeri, S., et al. (2024). Optimal harmonic resonance monitoring in electrical network considering area of harmonic pollution and system uncertainty. IET GTD.
key_references.relevance: Supports uncertainty-aware resonance monitoring and worst-case scenario considerations.

key_references.relevance: Evidence that operating conditions and compensation controls shift harmonic behavior and resonance risk.

key_references.relevance: Physical motivation for resonance amplification consequences and high-order harmonic stress.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Inconsistent timestamping and channel alignment across feeder and incomer records can bias proxy calculations.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Run alignment diagnostics (max lag, missing overlap, duplicate index check) and require all channels to pass pre-processing QC thresholds before feature generation.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Missing data and outlier contamination may destabilize stiffness and ramp proxies.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Compare interpolation/outlier policies via ablation tests; accept only if key metrics remain within predefined robustness bands across policies.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Proxy definitions may not faithfully represent true resonance mechanisms in all operating states.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Evaluate physical consistency signs and monotonicity checks; review coefficient directions and medoid behavior with domain constraints.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Cluster structure may be unstable with respect to feature scaling, k selection, and sampling window.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Assess clustering stability using repeated bootstrap runs, silhouette/gap consistency, and adjusted Rand index across perturbations.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Ordinal risk labels may be sensitive to threshold engineering assumptions.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Perform threshold perturbation analysis and report label transition rates, calibration drift, and class-imbalance effects.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Temporal leakage during model validation could inflate reported performance.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Use strictly forward-chaining time-series splits and leakage audits for feature windows and scaler fitting.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Limited transferability beyond the studied substation configuration.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Run external-like validation on held-out seasonal windows and document portability assumptions in REPRODUCIBILITY.md.

Risks & mitigations.risk: Private operational data governance may limit reproducibility in public CI.
Risks & mitigations.verification_method: Maintain reproducible synthetic/open benchmark pipeline path and verify artifact generation parity with private-data path where permitted.

User experience & accessibility.default_behavior: Default pipeline auto-runs preprocessing, proxy computation, PAM state reconstruction, and ordinal risk scoring with conservative thresholds and generates manuscript artifacts to manuscript/artifacts/.

run_log_schema.description: Per-stage execution logs for each pipeline run.

statistics_log_schema.description: Model performance and robustness statistics for reproducibility and audit.

Observability & logging.retention_policy: Retain run logs for 180 days and statistics logs for 365 days; rotate files at 25 MB with gzip archival.
Observability & logging.pii_redaction_rules: No personal identifiers expected; redact free-text fields, file-system usernames, and any accidental meter/customer identifiers before persistence.
